name: OpenHands
design_implication: Agent Foundry should expose installable local agent entrypoints, CLI/SDK-like execution surfaces, workspace runs, and release documentation rather than remaining only a dataset generator.

name: OpenHands GitHub
design_implication: Keep runnable source, issue-friendly artifacts, package verification, and local workspace execution traces as first-class release evidence.

name: OpenClaw
design_implication: A personal agent program should combine persistent memory, onboarding/persona setup, chat or CLI control, proactive work loops, and local ownership of context.

name: OpenClaw Docs
design_implication: The Talent Foundry public preview should document install, onboarding, skills/tools, background operation, and safety boundaries in a way users can inspect locally.

name: Reflexion
design_implication: Post-hire growth should promote reviewed verbal feedback and task outcomes into an episodic learning ledger without changing model weights by default.

name: Generative Agents
design_implication: The system should store observations, plans, reflections, and retrievable memory summaries so an AI talent develops a coherent reasoning style over time.

name: Survey on the Memory Mechanism of LLM-based Agents
design_implication: Agent memory needs explicit design and evaluation patterns, not just chat transcripts; audit gates should verify memory modules and quality controls.

name: From Human Memory to AI Memory
design_implication: The growth program should separate encoding, storage, retrieval, identity influence, and adaptation, while avoiding private chain-of-thought storage.

name: OpenClaw PRISM
design_implication: Tool-augmented local agents need defense-in-depth runtime boundaries, explicit authorization, and release-time safety audits.

name: Hermes Agent
design_implication: The public agent program should expose terminal, messaging, IDE, memory, skills, and profile concepts while keeping the talent identity separate from the LLM engine.

name: Hermes Memory Routing Issue
observed_problem: A monolithic memory file can increase prompt token overhead and make the agent load too much memory for the current task.
mitigation: Use indexed memory routing, small summaries, and on-demand sub-documents instead of injecting every long-term memory into each run.
design_implication: Talent Foundry memory should be accumulated through routed summaries and quality labels, not by copying every transcript into the active context.

name: Hermes Long-Session Field Report
observed_problem: Long-running local agent sessions can suffer context growth, noisy tool output, fragile compression, repeated memory failures, and unclear timeout states.
mitigation: Keep bounded traces, explicit job reports, acceptance checklists, and reviewed memory promotion so long sessions can be resumed without unlimited context growth.
design_implication: Every hired-agent job should leave compact artifacts and promote only reviewed outcomes into the reasoning kernel.

name: Hermes Profile Isolation Issue
observed_problem: Profile cloning and path-only isolation can leak or copy memory across supposedly separate agent profiles.
mitigation: Separate installed agent roots, employment records, learning ledgers, and profile paths; never infer identity from shared runtime folders alone.
design_implication: AI talents, projection swarms, and separately hired teams need explicit employment contexts and isolated local records.

name: OpenClaw Memory Index Issue
observed_problem: Memory indexing can fail for the main agent while empty agents appear healthy, leaving recall broken behind a misleading status.
mitigation: Audit memory source counts, required categories, and promotion decisions directly; treat unavailable memory tools as degraded rather than silently successful.
design_implication: Release audits should inspect memory evidence and degradation status instead of assuming that a configured memory backend is functioning.

name: OpenClaw Gateway CPU Field Report
observed_problem: Gateway process CPU and memory growth can make basic list/status calls slow enough to create request pileups.
mitigation: Prefer deterministic local demos, bounded JSONL logs, explicit doctor checks, and no background polling requirement for the first public preview.
design_implication: Talent Foundry should keep the first public preview runnable as a local CLI with small auditable artifacts before adding always-on gateway behavior.
